members.x-ms-description-for-model: Set to the other chat participants emails or user IDs separated by semicolons. User ID is Microsoft Entra ID user object IDs (GUIDs). You MUST NOT use raw names. Use either emails or the user IDs. Do not include the current USER's email or ID even if they ask to include them, as it's added automatically. Avoid duplicates. One ID or email creates a 1:1 chat; two or more create a group chat where the Title is shown. Example: alias@xyz.com;alias@abc.com

topic.description: Title, displayed only in group chats
topic.x-ms-description-for-model: Set to a short title for group chats only. Use a name that reflects the purpose. Leave empty if it is a 1 on 1 chat or if the name of the chat cannot reasonably be determined from request. Prefer capitalizing first letters unless user specifies topic. Example: Incident 1042, Q4 Planning Discussion

info.description: Microsoft Teams enables you to get all your content, tools and conversations in the team workspace with Microsoft 365.
info.title: Microsoft Teams

channelId__in_path.name: channelId
channelId__in_path.x-ms-description-for-model: Set the channel id within that team. Use the id returned by Teams (not the display name). Channel ids are often long strings starting with 19: and ending with @thread.tacv2. Do not use names like "General" unless already resolved to an id.

filter__odata.description: An OData filter query to restrict the entries returned (e.g. membershipType eq 'standard').
filter__odata.name: $filter

groupId__in_path.name: groupId
groupId__in_path.x-ms-description-for-model: Set the id of the team that owns the channel. Get it from configuration or a dynamic Team ID value from a prior tool like List teams. For shared channels, this must be the host team.

location__in_path.name: location
location__in_path.x-ms-description-for-model: Set to type of conversation where message should be posted. Valid values are "Group chat" (for group chats), "Channel" (for team channels), or "Chat with Flow bot" (for bot conversations). DO NOT select "Chat with Flow bot" if you set the poster property to be "User". If poster is Flow bot or Microsoft Copilot Studio agent and user does not specify location to post, default to "Chat with Flow bot".

orderby__odata.description: An OData orderBy query option for specifying the order of entries (e.g., displayName asc).
orderby__odata.name: $orderby

poster__in_path.name: poster
poster__in_path.x-ms-description-for-model: Set to the sender identity. Valid values are "User" (for messages from signed-in user), "Flow bot" (automated notifications), or "Microsoft Copilot Studio agent" (bot messages). Default to "User" if not specified.

recipientType__in_path.description: Type of the recipient of the action
recipientType__in_path.name: recipientType
recipientType__in_path.x-ms-summary: Type of the recipient of the action

topic__in_path.description: Filter by whether or not the topic name is defined
topic__in_path.name: topic

parameters.name: body
parameters.x-ms-description-for-model:
Set this to a structured object that defines the message content. At a minimum, include the content property and contentType (set to "html" or "text"). Example: { "content": "ALERT: Build #42 failed.
Check logs.", "contentType": "html" }

post.summary: Post message in a chat or channel

post.description: Construct a Microsoft Graph REST API request to invoke against the Microsoft Teams endpoints. parameters.name: Body

get.description: Triggers when a new message is posted to a channel in a team. Note that this trigger only fires when a root messages is added in the channel. Replies to an existing channel message will not result in the trigger event firing. For shared channels, the team ID must refer to the host team, which is the team that owns the shared channel.
get.summary: When a new channel message is added
get.x-ms-trigger-hint: To see it work now, add a message to the channel.

get.description: Triggers when a new message that @mentions the current user is added to a channel in a team. For shared channels, the team ID must refer to the host team, which is the team that owns the shared channel.
get.summary: When I am mentioned in a channel message
get.x-ms-trigger-hint: To see it work, get @mentioned in one of the specified channels.

get.description: Triggers when a member is added to the given team
get.summary: When a new team member is added
get.x-ms-trigger-hint: To see it work now, add a member to the team.

get.description: Triggers when a member is removed from the specified team
get.summary: When a new team member is removed
get.x-ms-trigger-hint: To see it work now, remove a member from the team.
